content:
🌟 AdGuardHome | Go
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide a network-wide solution for blocking advertisements and tracking on all connected devices by acting as a custom DNS server.
✨ Key Features: • Network-wide ads & trackers blocking • Operates as a DNS server • Blocks ads and tracking without client-side software • Supports DNS-over-HTTPS, DNS-over-TLS, DNS-over-QUIC, and DNSCrypt
📖 Summary: AdGuard Home is an open-source, network-wide DNS server designed to block ads and trackers across all devices on a home network. It functions by intercepting DNS requests and redirecting malicious domains to a null route, thereby preventing connections to unwanted servers without requiring client-side software.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

💡 webclaw | Rust
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide AI agents with clean, structured, and token-efficient web content extracted directly from URLs, bypassing common scraping obstacles.
✨ Key Features: • Fast, local-first web content extraction • Optimized output for LLMs (67% fewer tokens) • Sub-millisecond extraction speed • Zero browser overhead • Chrome-level TLS fingerprinting
📖 Summary: Webclaw is a high-performance web scraping tool written in Rust, designed for AI agents. It efficiently extracts structured data from web pages, optimizing content for LLM consumption by reducing token count and removing unnecessary elements, all without requiring a headless browser.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🚀 netscanner | Rust
🎯 Primary Use Case: Network scanning and diagnostics from the terminal with a modern TUI.
✨ Key Features: • List HW Interfaces • Switching active Interface • WiFi networks scanning • WiFi signals strength (with charts) • IPv4 Pinging CIDR
📖 Summary: Netscanner is a Rust-based terminal network scanner and diagnostic tool that provides a modern TUI for analyzing network interfaces, scanning WiFi networks, and performing packet dumping and port scanning. It allows users to switch active interfaces, monitor WiFi signal strength, ping IPv4 CIDRs, and filter packet logs.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

💡 inksight | Jupyter Notebook
🎯 Primary Use Case: Digitizing physical handwritten notes into searchable and editable digital formats.
✨ Key Features: • Offline-to-online handwriting conversion from photos • Multi-language support • Robust background handling • Word-level and full-page text processing • Vector-based digital ink output
📖 Summary: InkSight is a system that converts photos of handwritten text into editable digital ink. It uses a Vision Transformer and mT5 architecture to process diverse handwriting styles and backgrounds, supporting both word-level and full-page conversion for practical digitization.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🔥 awesome-bash | Shell
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide a comprehensive and curated collection of resources for learning, using, and mastering the Bash shell and its scripting capabilities.
✨ Key Features: • Curated list of Bash scripts and resources • Covers command-line productivity, customization, data handling, development tools, and more • Includes books, guides, FAQs, and best practices for Bash scripting • Links to related awesome lists for shell environments and tools
📖 Summary: Awesome Bash is a curated list of delightful Bash scripts and resources, aimed at enhancing command-line productivity, customization, and development. It serves as a comprehensive guide to learning and utilizing Bash effectively.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source
